segments.text: Hi, welcome to the ecosystem portion of
Sonar Summit. My name is Jeff Clawson. I
run our technology partnerships here at
Sonar. I've had the good fortune of
being in partnerships and strategic
alliances for the past 30 years and I've
done everything from cloud partnerships
to ISVs to integrators to resellers.
But what I can tell you is I've never
been as excited as I am about what we've
got going on here with Sonar and with
our partnerships. We've got so much
interest from partners, from customers.
The industry is moving in really
interesting directions and I'm excited
to talk to you a little bit today about
what we're doing, what we're building
and how this can benefit both our
partners and our customers. So I joined
Sonar a little over a year ago and when
I did we had built some very
one-directional partnerships where we
built integrations with partners to make
sure that we were inside the CI/CD
pipelines where that our customers were
using inside of the various IDE but we
weren't doing a whole lot of
collaboration back and forth between
our partners.
So, I can't tell you how many
conversations I had with partners in
those early days where they said, "Hey,
we reached out to Sonar. You told us you
didn't have a partner program. So, but
we are interested in doing more with
you." And I said, "Hey, look, here I am.
We're building a team around this. Let's
get going." And in the span of just that
short year, we've really gone
from zero to 60 and we've built out a
fantastic ecosystem of partners, many of
whom you're going to hear from today. In
fact, I told this story about these
partners and what they told
me to Daphna Diane who is the head
of partners and ecosystems over at Whiz
and she said, "Yep, we were one of those
one of those companies." And I'm excited
to say that we're heading down a path
with a on a great partnership with Whiz
that you'll hear more about in the not
distant future. But today, it's
really about these 14 partners. You
know, the ones that have spent the time
building out these integrations. They're
really the stars. I'm setting the stage
here and I hope that you have the chance
to sit down and listen to what they have
to say. So why does all this matter
right now? Why do we care about this
these partnerships, these
integrations? It's because the way the
code is getting developed today has
fundamentally changed. What has
traditionally been a very deterministic
model where developers sit down, they
spend weeks, months planning it out,
building it and developing is turning
into a very non-deterministic model with
these all these AI tools that are being
leveraged. AI is able to generate code
in milliseconds whereas humans it takes
minutes, hours, weeks to develop and
just that long to verify and validate
that code. And the problem is that speed
that we're seeing from the
AI development tools is happening
at such a rate that we're
creating tremendous scales of technical
debt by not being able to verify and
validate that code. So how do we
continue to move at this speed and what
are we going to do about it? We need to
embrace this speed but we need to make
sure that that verification keeps up. So
you've probably seen this many times.
Sonar talks about vibe then verify. We
want to make sure that you continue to
embrace the speed at which tools are
being developed, at which AI is enabling
us to create code, but we need to make
sure that that verification continues
throughout the process. And this is
really where Sonar's ecosystem comes
into play.
Customers, we are
making sure that sonar sits inside the
whole ecosystem of products whether it's
on the IDE side the pipeline side or the
governance side leveraging those
different tools you have access to the
sonar findings natively inside those
applications due to these integrations
that we are building with our partners
you don't have to flip from one
application to the other you don't have
to change context it all lives right
within there that's one of the key
things that we want to make sure that we
build is that seamless experience for
you as a customer partners. You're the
distribution layer for this trust. Look,
every integration that you build, it
extends the reach of verified code. You
take Sonar's intelligence and you put it
exactly where your developers need it.
And then for those AI teams, this
creates a set of guard rails for you and
gives you the confidence to continue to
move at speed without having to pump the
brakes, knowing that this is being
validated and verified real time and
that that code that you're sending out
is not going to be riddled with issues
and security holes.
We've effectively created a
plug-and-play with Sonar's MCP and APIs
for partners to create these
integrations that are seamlessly
accessible for our customers. And look,
I know that this sounds great in
concept, but let me show you how it
looks in practice. So, all of this
starts at the IDE. You're starting with
your planning, building, all your
tooling. We have built using our MCP
server into all of the AI
generation models whether it's cursor
wind surf anti-gravity you can use sonar
inside of all of those tools via the
MCP server. So let's say that
you're using cloud code it creates a snippet
that's completely unsanitized and it
puts a SQL injection risk into your
code. it looks clean to you, but
you know sonar an agent maybe that's running
sonar and keeping track of things. It'll
flag that inside the IDE and correct it
before you commit it. Don't have to
switch context.
And we are currently working with all of
the latest development tools whether it's
anti-gravity, cursor, VS code,
cloud code, windsurf, devon, we are
there regardless of which tools you are
using.
Now I want to talk a little bit about
the next step. So ID is the first line
but your code will move through a
pipeline. One of the first partnerships
that we established when I joined Sonar
was with Jrog. They came to us and said
hey we are building out our tool called
Artifactory and we see Sonar as a
critical piece of this because Sonar we
secure things at the code level and JROG
secures things at the binary level. is a
great meeting of the minds
together to be able to provide a
code to deployment type of solution. So
we worked very closely with JROG. Our
development teams got together. We
leveraged the APIs and built a very
seamless integration that allows for
sonar to verify the
code. JROG then verifies the artifact
and together they create a trust chain
from development all the way through
deployment and even more sonar
analysis evidence flows directly into
the JROG app trust so that the security
and devops team can see the trust score
of every artifact without even leaving
JROGs platform so it's all integrated
there makes it seamless and easy to use
for you as a customer look I'm not a
JROG expert you can expect to hear more
from them during one of the sessions and
I'd encourage you to watch and
hear what they have to say about
security in in today's AI development
world. So, Sonar wants to make sure that
we are inside all of the different tools
We talked about the IDE, how we're
inside the IDE,
cursor, VS Code, Windsor, anti-gravity.
We are also inside of all those pipeline
tools that you're going to be leveraging
either via API calls or MCP server,
GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket. And then
finally, one of the things that we're
seeing a lot of interest in from
partners is on the governance side.
We've had a large number of partners
come to us and say, "Hey, our customers
are asking for us to integrate sonar
into our governance platform." So we've
been working very closely with the likes
of Whiz, with Dinatrace, Jellyfish, and
many others, and I'm highlighting those
that you're going to hear today, hear
about today, but we have a very long
list of dozens of partners, and it's
going to be expanded even further who
have created integrations. You can find
more about that on our website.
But this allows us to be able to be that
glue inside of all of these applications
for the verification and validation of
all of your code. So regardless of what
tool you're using, you can
rely on Sonar to be able to validate and
verify all that code at the various
stages of your development life cycle.
And that workflow exists because of the
investment we've made in how partners
connect with Sonar. But that's
continuing to change and evolve. And I'd
like to tell you a little
bit about what we've got going. You
know, the market's in the midst of a
strategic shift. AI is forcing us all to
look at things differently. The way that
we develop code is a lot different. It
is creating tremendous technical debt if
it's not verified and validated at the
front end. We're going to continue to
focus on building integrations with our
APIs. You know, these birectional syncs
with CI/CD platforms. We're going to
continue to augment those APIs and
partners are going to continue to go
deep with Sonar with those APIs.
However, we need to start moving
quickly.
Sonar recently released our MCP server
and it's live. It's being leveraged by a
large number of our customers and a
large number of our partners including
Microsoft, Google, Cognition,
and many others. It's widely available
and solves a lot of this problem, a
lot of these problems that are required
to go quicker, but to be able to
validate and verify real time. It's with
the MCP that we really are able to scale
this out to increase the number of
partners that we have creating these
integrations to create the useful or to
create a more useful environment for
sonar. Let me share an example that my
development team brought to me recently.
As I mentioned, we're trying to figure
out how to make MCP a rapid development
process for creating these integrations.
In two days, they built what took them
literally three weeks to build previous.
What they did is they showed me a one of
these agentic AI development tools. I
don't remember if it was cursor or
anti-gravity. In any case, the AI agent
went and it built some code. Sonar then
flagged that in real time leveraging the
MCP server. There was a message that was
then generated in Slack and sent that to
Jira. Jira then wrote a ticket for that.
A claude agent was watching for those
tickets to be generated. It went out,
captured that ticket,
fixed the issue, sent a message back to
Slack, said it was fixed, and all this
was done hands off. Once again, built
this in a couple of days, but this is
the real power that this agentic
framework fixes for all of us. But what
I wanted to point out is Sonar sits
there at the middle of that. We
don't just have a onetoone
relationship with these partners. We're
able to sit across this whole
development life cycle and wherever you
are in it, whether it's agentic or human
run, we're able to ensure that that
verification and validation happens
regardless of where you are inside of
that cycle. So, let's talk a little bit
about the ecosystem that you're going to
hear from today. I've grouped these into
three different categories because
development happens at multiple
different stages. The first is that
ideation stage where you're going in
you're planning you're creating your
requirements document. Many of us
are using cloud code to do that. You're
going to be able to hear from anthropic
and their vision of this AI future and
how what it means for development.
Similarly, we've been working very
closely with OpenAI. They too have a
tremendous vision as does Google. I'd
encourage you to listen to all of these
and hear how that can apply to your
development journey. And then the
other one I wanted to point out was core
story. Course story has built a tool
that allows you to take your existing
legacy code and rapidly build a spec for
it and using AI take that and build and
refactor it into a new platform.
The other partners that we have coming
up are on that execution side. You
already heard me talk about JROG and the
Artifactory story and the integration
that we've built with them. You know,
GitHub and GitLab have a unique have
each their unique insights on what
they're doing in this agentic and AI
development space. Elassian, Accentia,
Cortex, all three of them are key
partners for us and we see them as
critical to our success and the
success of our partners as you leverage
their tools to do to do your
governance and application
development and management. And then
finally, you're going to be able to hear
from Whiz, Dinatrace, Jellyfish, and
Uba, who built tools that allow you to
expose those issues that Sonar is
raising up through your developers,
management, and see a 360 degree view of
what's happening inside of your
environment.
So, look, you're here to listen to them.
I just wanted to set the stage to make
sure that you could get excited about
what we're building. If you have any
applications as customers that you're
working with where you'd like to see a
better integration with Sonar, please
reach out and let us know. And partners,
those of you that want to build a deeper
integration with us or don't have one
yet, we also encourage you to reach out
and we'd love to build an integration
with you and create that partnership.
So, here's my key takeaway for today.
Continue to vibe, but also make sure you
verify, but don't just focus on speed
alone. Make sure you build for the trust
that makes the speed sustainable. Verify
that code and validate it throughout the
process. That's what we're building with
this ecosystem, a clean, succinct
process whereby sonar can be available
wherever you are in that process and
with whatever tool you're leveraging.
Look, we've got a lot of great companies
here that are building fantastic tools,
but in order for this to be a trusted
platform, an ecosystem, we need to
continue to verify that code together
from the first line to the very final
deployment. This ecosystem is going to
be critical to our success, to our
partner success, and to our customer
success as we continue to build together
in this environment that continues to
rapidly change.
Thanks again for joining us for Sonar
Summit. We're excited about the content
that we've provided. I'm so happy and
grateful for the partners and the time
that they've put into this. I think
you'll find their content extremely
valuable. Take the time to listen to it
either live or recorded. And feel free
to reach out to us if you have any
questions. And once again, thanks for
joining us.
